frameworks.name: LTP-like / LTD-like plasticity
frameworks.one_line: anodal tDCS over M1 induces synaptic-like plasticity reversible by intensity.

frameworks.name: counter-regulatory homeostatic mechanisms
frameworks.one_line: higher stimulation intensities trigger compensatory reversal of excitability changes.

frameworks.name: corticospinal excitability via TMS-MEP
frameworks.one_line: single- and paired-pulse TMS index intracortical excitation and inhibition.

core_question: What anodal tDCS intensity threshold reverses the direction of M1 plasticity?
key_assumptions: a-tDCS over M1 normally induces LTP-like plasticity
CSE measured by TMS-MEP reflects motor cortex plasticity
ICF, SICI, LICI index distinct intracortical circuits
manipulations: timepoint of CSE assessment (pre, immediately post, 30-min post)
key_findings: CSE increased significantly at 0.7 mA a-tDCS
Expected facilitation decreased and reversed at 1 and 1.5 mA
ICF increased and SICI/LICI decreased at 0.7 mA
ICF decreased and SICI/LICI increased at 1 and 1.5 mA
interpretations: Intensity threshold of >=1 mA for 26 min reverses LTP- into LTD-like plasticity.
Counter-regulatory intracortical inhibition mechanisms underlie the reversal.
